title: Systems and methods for electrochemical generation of syngas and other useful chemicals
abstract: Processes and apparatus for electrocatalytically converting carbon dioxide emissions and/or ambient carbon dioxide into useful chemicals are described. The process may include: removing carbon dioxide from ambient air through a carbon capture technique, supplying a carbonate or bicarbonate aqueous solution as cathode feed to a cathode of an electrolytic cell comprising a membrane electrode assembly which includes a bipolar membrane separating an anode from the cathode, and applying an electrical potential difference between the cathode and the anode of the membrane electrode assembly to electrocatalytically reduce the carbonate or bicarbonate aqueous solution to carbon monoxide or another useful chemical.
claims: Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1 . A carbon capture method comprising: absorbing and chemically reacting gaseous carbon dioxide to form bicarbonate and/or carbonate in an aqueous solution; supplying the aqueous solution at a cathode of an electrochemical reactor comprising an anode and the cathode separated by a bipolar membrane and applying a potential difference between the anode and the cathode to cause an electrochemical reaction yielding product gas comprising one or both of gas phase carbon dioxide and gas phase carbon monoxide; and separating the product gas from the aqueous solution. 2. The method according to claim 1 comprising, at the cathode of the electrochemical reactor, generating hydrogen gas wherein the product gas comprises the hydrogen gas. 3. The method according to claim 1 or claim 2 wherein the aqueous solution comprises bicarbonate at a concentration of at least 3 moles/liter. 4. The method according to any one of claims 1 to 3 wherein a current flowing in the electrochemical reactor as a result of the applied potential has a current density at the cathode of at least 100 mA/cm 2 . 5. The method according to any one of claims 1 to 4 wherein the electrochemical reactor is operated at a temperature not exceeding 150 C and not exceeding a boiling point of the aqueous solution. 6. The method according to any one of claims 1 to 5 comprising maintaining a pressure of the aqueous solution at the cathode of the electrochemical reactor at 2 atmospheres or less. 7. The method according to any one of claims 1 to 6 comprising flowing the aqueous solution through a cathode chamber of the electrochemical reactor. 8. The method according to any one of claims 1 to 8 wherein the aqueous solution has a pH above 7. 9. The method according to claim 8 wherein the aqueous solution comprises a strong base. 10. The method according to claim 8 or 9 wherein the pH of the aqueous solution is at least 8.0. 1 1 . The method according to any one of claims 8 to 10 wherein the pH of the aqueous solution when supplied for chemically reacting the gaseous carbon dioxide is in the range of 8 to 10. 12. The method according to any one of claims 1 to 1 1 wherein the aqueous solution has a first pH at an input of the electrochemical reactor and a second pH at an output of the electrochemical reactor and the second pH is higher than the first pH. 13. The method according to any one of claims 1 to 12 comprising controlling a composition of the product gas by adjusting a magnitude of the potential difference. 14. The method according to claim 13 wherein a concentration of C0 2 in the aqueous solution is below 7 mM. 15. The method according to any one of claims 1 to 14 wherein the product gas comprises C0 2 , CO and H 2 . 16. The method according to claim 15 wherein the product gas comprises no more than 50% C0 2 . 17. The method according to claim 15 or 16 wherein a molar ratio of CO to H 2 in the product gas is greater than 1 . 18. The method according to claim 15 or 16 wherein a molar ratio of CO to H 2 in the product gas is less than 1 . 19. The method according to any one of claims 1 to 18 wherein absorbing and chemically reacting the gaseous carbon dioxide comprises flowing the gaseous carbon dioxide through a filter wetted by the aqueous solution. 20. The method according to any one of claims 1 to 19 wherein the aqueous solution comprises a caustic alkaline solution. 21 . The method according to any one of claims 1 to 20 wherein the aqueous solution comprises an enzyme that is active for promoting the chemical reaction of the carbon dioxide to yield bicarbonate and/or carbonate. 22. The method according to claim 21 wherein the enzyme comprises a carbonic anhydrase enzyme catalyst. 23. The method according to any one of claims 1 to 22 comprising circulating the aqueous solution back to absorb and chemically react with more gaseous carbon dioxide after separating the product gas from the aqueous solution. 24. Apparatus for carbon capture, the apparatus comprising: a contactor having a fluid inlet and a fluid outlet, the contactor configured to bring a gas comprising carbon dioxide into contact with an aqueous solution provided at the fluid inlet; an electrochemical reactor comprising an anode and the cathode separated by a bipolar membrane and a power supply connected to apply a potential difference between the anode and the cathode, a cathode side of the electrochemical reactor comprising a fluid inlet and a fluid outlet wherein the fluid outlet of the contactor is in fluid communication with the fluid inlet of the electrochemical reactor such that the aqueous solution is delivered to the electrochemical reactor after contacting the gas; and a product gas separator located to collect product gas produced by the electrochemical reactor. 25. The apparatus according to claim 24 wherein the contactor and electrochemical reactor are connected in a recirculation loop such that the aqueous solution is delivered back to the contactor after exiting the electrochemical reactor. 26. The apparatus according to claim 24 or 25 wherein the aqueous solution comprises a strong base. 27. The apparatus according to claim 26 wherein the aqueous solution comprises a solution of KOH or NaOH. 28. The apparatus according to any one of claims 24 to 27 wherein the aqueous solution has a pH of at least 8 at the fluid inlet of the contactor. 29. The apparatus according to any one of claims 24 to 27 wherein the aqueous solution has a pH in the range of 8 to 10 at the fluid inlet of the contactor. 30. The apparatus according to any one of claims 24 to 29 wherein the aqueous solution comprises an enzyme catalyst. 31 . The apparatus according to claim 30 wherein the enzyme catalyst comprises a carbonic anhydrase. 32. The apparatus according to any one of claims 24 to 31 wherein the concentration of the carbonate and/or bicarbonate in the aqueous solution at the fluid inlet of the electrochemical reactor is at least 0.5M. 33. The apparatus according to any one of claims 24 to 32 wherein the product gas separator comprises a chamber having a gas outlet at an upper end of the chamber. 34. The apparatus according to any one of claims 24 to 33 wherein the cathode is in contact with the bipolar membrane. 35. The apparatus according to any one of claims 24 to 34 wherein the cathode comprises a porous gas diffusion layer. 36. The apparatus according to any one of claims 24 to 35 comprising a cathode catalyst at the cathode wherein the cathode catalyst is preferably one or both of silver and gold. 37. A method for processing a solution of bicarbonate or carbonate to yield one or more carbon compounds, the method comprising: supplying an aqueous solution comprising bicarbonate and/or carbonate at a cathode of an electrochemical reactor comprising an anode and the cathode separated by a bipolar membrane; and applying a potential difference between the anode and the cathode to cause an electrochemical reaction yielding product gas comprising one or both of gas phase carbon dioxide and gas phase carbon monoxide; and separating the product gas from the aqueous solution; wherein the electrochemical reactor is operated at a pressure not exceeding 2 atmospheres. 38. Apparatus having any new and inventive feature, combination of features, or sub-combination of features as described herein. 39. Methods having any new and inventive steps, acts, combination of steps and/or acts or sub-combination of steps and/or acts as described herein.
